topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the entryway table, a stark splash of offensively bright colors against the muted beige of the hallway. I didn't touch it. My jaw was tight enough to crack enamel. These ridiculously cheerful lilies, the kind that scream 'sentimental nonsense,' had appeared out of nowhere. No note. Just stems wrapped in cellophane. I stared at the arrangement, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the nearby console. A slow, simmering heat rose in my chest, a pressure building behind my eyes. Who thought this was funny? Who decided to send such a blatant display of misplaced sentiment? I wanted to snatch the box, to crumple the paper, to make a scene. Instead, I just stood there, breathing in shallow, ragged gasps, my entire body vibrating with a tightly coiled tension.

[story 2]

"Are you serious?" I hissed, the words barely escaping my throat. The roses, deep crimson and dripping with an almost saccharine perfume, lay on the counter like a bizarre, unwanted offering. I picked one up, the petals cool and slick beneath my fingertips, and immediately dropped it again. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary intake of air. My hands clenched into fists so tight my nails dug crescent moons into my palms. Every nerve ending felt exposed, buzzing with a sharp, unwelcome energy. I paced the length of the living room, the floorboards protesting under my hurried steps. This was an insult, a deliberate, baffling gesture designed solely to derail my composure. I needed to find the sender, needed to dissect the audacity of it all, but all I could manage was a rigid, trembling stillness, radiating pure, contained aggravation.

[story 3]

I walked into the kitchen, the silence amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears. There, on the pristine white granite, was the bouquet. They were vibrant, almost aggressively happy, and utterly devoid of context. My eyes narrowed, scanning the arrangement as if it held some hidden, malicious code. A low growl rumbled deep in my core, a sound I barely recognized. I slammed the refrigerator door shut, the sudden noise echoing the violent clench in my stomach. I leaned against the counter, crossing my arms tightly across my chest, attempting to physically compress the rising tide of irritation. Every muscle in my neck was taut, rigid. I wanted to scream at the universe for sending this baffling, unsolicited display, but the sound caught in my throat, leaving only a tight, vibrating silence that promised a reckoning.
